steps.title: Identify the sine ratio and find AC
steps.content:
In right triangle ABC with the right angle at C, the sine of angle B equals the ratio of the opposite side to the hypotenuse. From the diagram, AB (the hypotenuse) = 26, so:
$$\sin(B) = \frac{AC}{AB}$$
Substituting the known values:
$$\frac{5}{13} = \frac{AC}{26}$$
Multiply both sides by 26:
$$AC = 26 \cdot \frac{5}{13} = \frac{130}{13} = 10$$

steps.title: Use the Pythagorean theorem to find BC
steps.content:
Now that we know AC = 10 and AB = 26, apply the Pythagorean theorem to the right triangle:
$$AC^2 + BC^2 = AB^2$$
$$10^2 + BC^2 = 26^2$$
$$100 + BC^2 = 676$$
$$BC^2 = 576$$
$$BC = \sqrt{576} = 24$$
